claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you have?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I have two children - Alex, who is 9, and Blake, who is 5. They both live with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex (age 9) and Blake (age 5), and they both live with you at home.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

**Are you or your partner currently receiving Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently receiving Child Benefit for either of them, and neither is anyone else as far as I'm aware.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Next question: **Are you responsible for Alex and Blake? By this I mean, do you provide for their care and they live with you regularly?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for both of them - they live with me full-time and I provide for all their care and needs.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming.

Next, I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you.

Now I need to ask: **Are either Alex or Blake currently in local authority care, or have they been in hospital or residential care for more than 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in care or hospital. They're both at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One more important question: **Do you have an immigration status that allows you to claim benefits in the UK?** For example, are you a British citizen, have settled status, or have the right to reside and claim benefits in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have the right to claim benefits in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing all that information.

Based on what you've told me, let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake:

**You appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's why:

✓ **Alex (age 9):** You are responsible for Alex, who lives with you full-time. Alex is under 16 years old, not in local authority care, and you live in the UK with the right to claim benefits. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria.

✓ **Blake (age 5):** You are responsible for Blake, who lives with you full-time. Blake is under 16 years old, not in local authority care, and you live in the UK with the right to claim benefits. Blake meets all the eligibility criteria.

You should be able to make a claim for Child Benefit for both children. You'll receive a higher rate for your eldest qualifying child (Alex) and a lower rate for your second child (Blake).

**Important to note:** If you or your partner earn over £60,000 a year, you can still claim Child Benefit, but you may need to pay a tax charge (the High Income Child Benefit Charge) if either of you earns over that amount.

Would you like any further information about Child Benefit or the application process?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's brilliant, thank you so much for your help! This is really clear and helpful. I think I have all the information I need now.
